title: GSRF: Complex-Valued 3D Gaussian Splatting for Efficient Radio-Frequency Data Synthesis

repos.name: nesl__GSRF
repos.evidence: git origin=github.com/nesl/GSRF (NESL = Mani Srivastava's UCLA lab, the paper's last author); latest commit by kangyang73 <yangkangyk@hotmail.com> = first author Kang Yang. README describes GSRF on the exact three datasets RFID/BLE/CSI the paper evaluates and says it is 'adapted from 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) by GraphDECO/Inria', matching the paper's 'extends 3DGS'. utils/fle_utils.py implements the Fourier-Legendre Expansion (contribution 1); submodules/complex-gaussian-tracer{,-csi} are real CUDA kernels (189-line tracer_points.cu) for complex-valued ray tracing (contribution 3); scene/gaussian_model.py (566 lines) is the complex-valued Gaussian model; train_rfid.py/main_ble.py/main_csi.py + three inference_*.py cover the paper's RFID/BLE/CSI experiments.

repos.name: nesl__GSRF/submodules/simple-knn
repos.evidence: Standard simple-knn CUDA extension bundled from the Inria 3DGS codebase; installed via README's 'pip install -e submodules/simple-knn'. Part of the core repo's proper build, not a separately-cloned wrong repo.

rationale: Exactly one repo was cloned (nesl/GSRF) and it is unambiguously the paper's own core implementation: repo owner (NESL/UCLA, last author Srivastava) and top committer (Kang Yang, first author) match the author list; the README and code implement the paper's three named contributions (Fourier-Legendre radiance via fle_utils.py, complex-valued CUDA ray tracing via the complex-gaussian-tracer submodules, complex-valued Gaussian model) on the same three RF datasets (RFID/BLE/CSI). No baseline/different-paper/stub repo was mis-grabbed. The [title-match] prior in code_links.txt is correct here (it is the author repo, not a like-named library).
audit_impact: No provenance error. The audit correctly treated nesl/GSRF as the paper's own code and its findings are scoped, accurate partial-completeness observations (missing baseline NeRF2/WRF-GS/R2F2/FIRE code, missing Table-1 ablation toggles, missing BLE localization and measurement-density experiments, FLE-degree config=9 vs paper L=3). These are NOT 'no code exists' claims written against a wrong repo; they hold against the genuine core. No finding needs to be retracted on provenance grounds; verdicts stand.
recommended_action: No reclassification needed. Keep paper as source-present / core-present. The high-severity 'missing baseline/ablation code' findings are legitimate against the real author repo and can remain as completeness gaps (authors should supply baseline + ablation + localization + density-sweep scripts and reconcile fle_degree config with the paper's L=3 claim).
